A NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED THAT A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF THE LAKESIDE GOLF CLUB NORTH OF FM 2673. THE TORNADO TRACKED SOUTHEAST APPROXIMATELY THREE MILES, DAMAGING AND UPROOTING TREES AT EF-0 INTENSITY ALONG ITS PATH. MINOR ROOF DAMAGE WAS NOTED AT RESIDENCES ALONG CAMPBELL DRIVE AND DOROTHY DRIVE. THE TORNADO TRAVELED SOUTHEAST CROSSING FM 2673 NEAR THE INTERSECTION WITH FM 2722 BEFORE LIFTING NEAR JBJ DRIVE. PEAK WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE 85 MPH. OTHER SPORADIC STRAIGHT-LINE WIND DAMAGE WAS ALSO NOTED AROUND THE CANYON LAKE AREA.

...Delayed Report...A National Weather Service damage survey concluded that a small EF0 tornado occurred north-northwest of Stockdale, TX during the early morning hours of October 26th. The tornado touched down roughly 3 miles north-northwest of Stockdale, near County Road 422, producing a narrow path of tree damage. The tornado continued north, remaining disorganized and producing sporadic tree damage. At times the path was hard to determine between Country Road 334 and Country Road 434, and the tornado may have lifted off the ground at times. Along Longhorn Road the tornado produced damage to a patio awning of a residence, tore tin roofs off sheds and pens, and produced additional tree damage. The tornado dissipated just north of Longhorn Road. Maximum winds of 75 mph were estimated, with a patch length of 6 miles.
Very preliminary report. NWS Survey Team will be out Friday morning to establish the full tornado path length, width, and damage rating.

...Delayed Report...a National Weather Service Storm survey, along with resident video and pictures, has concluded that a weak EF0 tornado touched down in Cibolo Texas the morning of October 26. There was some tree and fence damage in the Falcon Ridge neighborhood. The tornado moved NE and caused some shingle damage on several homes in the Saratoga neighborhood. There were several areas along the path where no damage was found. The maximum winds are estimated at 65 mph with a path length of 2 miles.

...Delayed Report....A National Weather Service storm survey concluded a small EF0 tornado briefly occurred on FM306 near Purgatory Road. Maximum winds are estimated at 70 mph. There was some tree damage and minor damage to a few residences in that area. It appears to have been on the ground for about one quarter mile.
Very preliminary report. NWS Survey Team will be out Friday to establish if damage was caused by a tornado or straight line winds.
